the film — Watch the K Foundation Burn a Million Quid — was toured around the UK.

the original production of Kinky Boots premiered at the Bank of America Theatre in Chicago in October 2012.

the show deals with the theme of death.

topic.name: World War II Naval Warfare
topic.description: The documents discuss various naval vessels and their roles in World War II, focusing on their design, operations, and historical significance.

Document 1: Admiral Scheer was a Deutschland-class heavy cruiser which served with the Kriegsmarine of Nazi Germany during World War II.
Her first operation during World War II was a commerce raiding operation into the southern Atlantic.
Document 2: Zuihō was a light aircraft carrier of the Imperial Japanese Navy.
The ship participated in many operations during World War II.
Document 3: Satsuma was a semi-dreadnought battleship built for the Imperial Japanese Navy in the first decade of the 20th century.
She saw no combat during World War I, although she led a squadron that occupied several German colonies in the Pacific Ocean in 1914.
Document 4: The Japanese aircraft carrier Unryū was the lead ship of her class of fleet aircraft carriers built for the Imperial Japanese Navy during World War II.
She was torpedoed and sunk by the American submarine USS Redfish in the East China Sea during the voyage.
Document 5: The Asama-class cruisers were a pair of armored cruisers built for the Imperial Japanese Navy in the late 1890s.
They participated in all four main naval battles of the Russo-Japanese War of 1904 – 05.
Document 6: Mikasa is a pre-dreadnought battleship built for the Imperial Japanese Navy in the late 1890s.
She served as the flagship of Admiral Tōgō Heihachirō throughout the Russo-Japanese War of 1904 – 1905.
Document 7: The Zuihō class was a group of two aircraft carriers built for the Imperial Japanese Navy before World War II.
Both ships were originally built as submarine tenders, but were subsequently converted into carriers.

topic.name: State Highways in Michigan and Ohio
topic.description: The documents discuss various state highways in Michigan and Ohio, detailing their routes, history, and geographic significance.

Document 1: M-311 is a 13.7-mile-long state trunkline highway in Calhoun County in the US state of Michigan
M-311 follows 11 Mile Road between M-60 and I-94.
Document 2: State Route 368 is a short north – south state highway in western Ohio
SR 368 travels from its southern terminus at SR 366 approximately 1.75 miles east of Russells Point.
Document 3: M-201 is a state trunkline highway in the US state of Michigan
The trunkline zig-zags through to provide a path through the village.
Document 4: State Route 372 is a very short, two-lane east – west state highway in the southern part of the U.S. state of Ohio
The primary function of SR 372 has been to provide access to the state forest from the state highway system.
Document 5: M-188 is a 4.559-mile-long state trunkline highway in the US state of Michigan
M-188 heads eastward from the intersection with M-99 / M-50.
Document 6: M-209 was a state trunkline highway in the lower peninsula of the US state of Michigan
M-209 started at M-109 and went 0.543 miles to Glen Haven.

topic.name: Music Albums and Artists
topic.description: The documents discuss various music albums and their artists, highlighting their production, themes, and critical reception.

Document 1: FutureSex / LoveSounds is the second studio album by American singer-songwriter Justin Timberlake.
The album produced six singles that attained chart success.
Document 2: Voliminal : Inside the Nine is the third video album by American metal band Slipknot.
The DVD was promoted on various websites for the weeks leading up to its limited theatrical release.
Document 3: Ruby Blue is the debut solo album of Irish singer Róisín Murphy.
The album often samples sounds made by everyday objects and actions.
Document 4: Neon Bible is the second studio album by the Canadian rock band Arcade Fire.
Critics offered the self-produced Neon Bible mostly favorable reviews.
Document 5: Circus is the sixth studio album by American recording artist Britney Spears.
The album topped charts in nine additional countries.
Document 6: The Christmas Sessions is the first Christmas album by American Christian rock band MercyMe.
The album reached a peak of number three on the Billboard Christian Albums chart.
Document 7: OverClocked ReMix is a non-commercial organization dedicated to preserving and paying tribute to video game music.
The primary focus of OC ReMix is its website which offers thousands of free fan-made video game music arrangements.

topic.name: Historical Cricketers
topic.description: The documents discuss the careers and achievements of notable cricketers from different eras and regions.

Document 1: Alan Kippax was a cricketer for New South Wales (NSW) and Australia.
Kippax is best remembered for a performance in domestic cricket — a world record last wicket partnership.
Document 2: Arnold Fothergill was an English professional cricketer who played first-class cricket for Somerset and the Marylebone Cricket Club (MCC).
Fothergill took 119 first-class wickets during his career, of which eight came in his two Tests.
Document 3: Virat Kohli is an Indian international cricketer and often rated as one of the best batsmen in the world.
Kohli captained India Under-19s to victory at the 2008 Under-19 World Cup.
Document 4: George Dewhurst was a Trinidadian cricketer who played for West Indies before the team attained Test match status.
Dewhurst was an influential and popular member of the Trinidad and West Indian sides.
Document 5: Somerset County Cricket Club made their debut in first-class cricket in the 1882 English cricket season.
The club struggled to remain financially solvent and had no fixed home ground.

topic.name: Historical Tropical Storms and Hurricanes
topic.description: This topic encompasses significant historical tropical storms and hurricanes that have impacted various regions, particularly in the United States, detailing their development, intensity, and effects.

Document 1: Tropical Storm Chris caused minor flooding along the Gulf Coast of the United States in September 1982.
The storm caused steady rainfall in parts of the Midwestern United States.
Document 2: The 1893 New York hurricane was a powerful and destructive tropical cyclone that struck the New York City area in August 1893.
The storm inflicted severe damage with storm tides as high as 30 ft.
Document 3: Hurricane Gabrielle was a North Atlantic hurricane that caused flooding in both Florida and Newfoundland in September 2001.
The storm caused about $ 230 million in damage in Florida.
Document 4: Tropical Storm Bonnie was a weak tropical storm that brought squally weather to the northern Caribbean Sea and Gulf Coast of the United States in July 2010.
The effects were more severe from the remnants of Bonnie.
Document 5: Tropical Storm Debra was the second of two tropical storms to hit the United States in the 1978 Atlantic hurricane season.
The storm made landfall on the coast of Louisiana, east of the Texas border.
Document 6: Tropical Storm Danny was a weak and disorganized tropical cyclone that formed in August 2009.
Localized flooding was reported, particularly in Pennsylvania.
Document 7: The effects of Hurricane Dean in Mexico were more severe than anywhere else in the storm's path.
Hurricane Dean made landfall on Mexico's Yucatán Peninsula as a catastrophic Category 5 hurricane.

topic.name: Fungal Species and Taxonomy
topic.description: The documents discuss various species of fungi, their characteristics, taxonomy, and ecological relationships.

Document 1: Lactifluus piperatus is a semi-edible basidiomycete fungus of the genus Lactifluus.
It forms a symbiotic relationship with various species of deciduous tree.
Document 2: Pilophorus acicularis is a species of lichen in the Cladoniaceae family.
It grows directly on silicate rocks in dense clusters.
Document 3: Amanita abrupta is a species of fungus in the Amanitaceae family of mushrooms.
This terrestrial species grows in mixed woods in eastern North America and eastern Asia.
Document 4: Psilocybe allenii is a species of agaric fungus in the family Hymenogastraceae.
It contains the psychoactive compounds psilocin and psilocybin.
Document 5: Psilocybe yungensis is a species of psychedelic mushroom in the Strophariaceae family.
It is used by Mazatec Indians in the Mexican State of Oaxaca for entheogenic purposes.
Document 6: Podoserpula is a genus of fungi in the family Amylocorticiaceae.
The genus contains two species: the type, P. pusio, commonly known as the pagoda fungus.

topic.name: Medieval and Early Modern Castles in England
topic.description: This topic encompasses the historical significance, architectural styles, and cultural heritage of medieval and early modern castles in England, highlighting their roles in defense and tourism.

Document 1: Nunney Castle is a medieval castle at Nunney in the English county of Somerset.
The architectural style, possibly influenced by the design of French castles, has provoked considerable academic debate.
Document 2: Sandsfoot Castle, also known historically as Weymouth Castle, is an artillery fort constructed by Henry VIII near Weymouth, Dorset.
It formed part of the King 's Device programme to protect against invasion from France and the Holy Roman Empire.
Document 4: The Grade I listed buildings in Somerset demonstrate the history and diversity of its architecture.
Grade I structures are those considered to be 'buildings of exceptional interest'.
Document 7: St Catherine 's Castle is a Henrician castle in Cornwall, built by Thomas Treffry between approximately 1538 and 1540.
It was built in response to fears of an invasion of England by France and the Holy Roman Empire.

topic.name: American Political Figures
topic.description: The documents discuss various American political figures and their contributions or roles in politics throughout different historical periods.

1: Gary Johnson is an American businessman, politician and the Libertarian Party nominee for President of the United States in the 2016 election.
He served as the 29th Governor of New Mexico from 1995 to 2003 as a member of the Republican Party.
2: Jane Fonda is an American actress, writer, political activist, former fashion model and fitness guru.
She is a two-time Academy Award winner and two time BAFTA Award winner.
3: General Joel Palmer was an American pioneer of the Oregon Territory.
He served as a member of the Indiana House of Representatives.
4: Thomas Cushing III was an American lawyer, merchant, and statesman from Boston, Massachusetts.
He represented Massachusetts in the First and Second Continental Congresses.
5: William Jason Fields was a politician from the U.S. state of Kentucky.
He represented Kentucky's Ninth District in the U.S. House of Representatives from 1911 to 1923.

topic.name: American Sports History
topic.description: The documents discuss notable figures and events in American sports, highlighting achievements in football, basketball, and other athletic competitions.

1: Everett Strupper was an American football player and selected as an All-American in 1917.
Strupper was posthumously inducted into the College Football Hall of Fame in 1972.
2: Jordan Kovacs is an American football safety who played college football at Michigan.
Kovacs earned 2009 CollegeFootballNews.com Freshman All-America second team recognition.
6: Sarah Vinci is a 1 point wheelchair basketball player who represented Australia at the 2012 Summer Paralympics in London.
Vinci joined the Perth Western Stars in the Women 's National Wheelchair Basketball League (WNWBL) in 2009.

topic.name: Historical Figures and Events
topic.description: The documents discuss notable historical figures and their contributions or controversies across different time periods and contexts.

1: Sally Hemings was an enslaved woman of mixed race owned by President Thomas Jefferson.
The historical question of whether Jefferson was the father of Hemings' children is known as the Jefferson – Hemings controversy.
2: Reginald fitz Jocelin was a medieval Bishop of Bath and an Archbishop of Canterbury-elect in England.
He was a household clerk for Thomas Becket, but by 1167 he was serving King Henry II of England.
3: Louis McHenry Howe was an American reporter for the New York Herald best known for acting as an early political advisor to President Franklin D. Roosevelt.
Howe oversaw Roosevelt's campaign for the New York State Senate.
4: Franz Ritter von Hipper was an admiral in the German Imperial Navy.
He is most famous for commanding the German battlecruisers of the I Scouting Group during World War I.
5: I Corps is a corps of the United States Army headquartered in Joint Base Lewis-McChord, Washington.
Activated in World War I in France, I Corps oversaw US Army divisions as they repelled several major German offensives.
6: The 25th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the New Zealand Military Forces, which served during the Second World War.
It participated in the North African Campaign and suffered heavy losses during Operation Crusader.
7: Gabriel García Márquez was a Colombian novelist, short-story writer, screenwriter and journalist, known affectionately as Gabo.
He was awarded the 1982 Nobel Prize in Literature.

topic.name: Public Transportation Systems
topic.description: The documents collectively discuss various public transportation systems, including trams, railways, and commuter services in different geographical locations.

Document 1: Edinburgh Trams is a tramway in Edinburgh, Scotland, operated by Transport for Edinburgh.
Construction began in June 2008, and it opened on 31 May 2014.
Document 2: The Follo Line is a planned high-speed railway between Oslo and Ski, Norway.
Construction is estimated to start in 2014, and may be completed by 2020/21.
Document 3: Shore Line East is a commuter rail service which operates along the Northeast Corridor through southern Connecticut, US.
The service was introduced in 1990 as a temporary measure to reduce congestion during construction work on I-95.
Document 4: The Mountaineer was a passenger train operated by Amtrak between Norfolk, Virginia, and Chicago, Illinois.
Service began in 1975 and ended in 1977.
Document 5: The Roa – Hønefoss Line is a 32-kilometer long, single track railway line between Roa and Hønefoss in Norway.
The line was electrified in 1961.
Document 6: The Gardermoen Line is a high-speed railway line between Oslo and Eidsvoll, Norway.
The line was opened in 1998, at the same time as the airport that gave the line its name.
Document 7: The Capitol Limited was an American passenger train run by the Baltimore and Ohio Railroad.
It was the B & O's flagship passenger train, noted for personalized service and innovation.

topic.name: Diverse Influences in Media and Culture
topic.description: This topic encompasses the impact of various cultural and media figures, events, and products across different sectors including automotive, entertainment, sports, and historical events.

Document 1: Nokian Tyres plc, headquartered in Nokia, Finland, produces tyres for cars, trucks, buses, and heavy-duty equipment.
Known for its winter tyres, Nokian operates the only permanent winter tyre testing facility in the world.
Document 2: On My Way is the fourteenth episode and winter finale of the third season of the American musical television series Glee.
The episode received a strong and varied reaction from reviewers.
Document 3: David B. Falk is an American sports agent who primarily works with basketball players in the National Basketball Association.
Falk has represented more than 100 other NBA players, and is generally considered to be the most influential player agent the NBA has seen.
Document 4: The Lexus LS is a full-size luxury car serving as the flagship model of Lexus, the luxury division of Toyota.
Development of the LS began in 1983 as the F1 project, the code name for a secret flagship sedan.
Document 5: Raemer Edgar Schreiber was an American physicist from McMinnville, Oregon who served Los Alamos National Laboratory during World War II.
He participated in the development of the atomic bomb.
Document 6: Alexander Litvinenko was a former officer of the Russian Federal Security Service who fled from court prosecution in Russia.
His public deathbed accusations resulted in worldwide media coverage.
Document 7: Barnard Barney Gumble is a fictional character on the American animated sitcom The Simpsons.
Barney was inspired by the cartoon character Barney Rubble from The Flintstones.

topic.name: Dog and Horse Breeds History
topic.description: This topic encompasses the historical development, characteristics, and significance of various dog and horse breeds, particularly those originating in specific regions.

Document 1: The French Spaniel is a breed of dog of the Spaniel-like setter.
It was developed in France and Canada as a hunting dog.
Document 2: A Dandie Dinmont Terrier is a small Scottish breed of dog in the terrier family.
The breed originates from the dogs being used in the border country of Scotland and England.
Document 3: The Miniature Schnauzer is a breed of small dog of the Schnauzer type that originated in Germany.
They were originally bred to be medium-sized farm dogs in Germany.
Document 4: The Narragansett Pacer was the first horse breed developed in the United States.
It was developed from a mix of English and Spanish breeds.
Document 5: The Morgan horse is one of the earliest horse breeds developed in the United States.
Morgans have influenced other major American breeds.
Document 6: The Canadian horse is a horse breed from Canada.
It was later crossed with other British and American breeds.
Document 7: The Boreray is a breed of sheep originating on the St Kilda archipelago off the west coast of Scotland.
The breed is the rarest breed of sheep in the United Kingdom.

topic.name: Bacon and Fast Food Innovations
topic.description: This topic encompasses the creative and experimental uses of bacon in modern culinary innovations, particularly within the fast food industry.

Document 1: Bacon ice cream is a modern invention, generally created by adding bacon to egg custard and freezing the mixture.
The concept of bacon ice cream originated in a 1973 sketch on the British comedy series The Two Ronnies as a joke.
Document 2: The Whopper is the signature hamburger product sold by the international fast-food restaurant chain Burger King.
The company markets several variants of the burger as well as other variants that are specifically tailored to meet local taste preferences.
Document 3: The Burger King Specialty Sandwiches are a line of sandwiches developed by the international fast-food restaurant chain Burger King in 1978.
Burger King markets the Original Chicken Sandwich under several different names globally.
Document 6: A Bacon Explosion is a pork dish that consists of bacon wrapped around a filling of spiced sausage and crumbled bacon.
The Bacon Explosion became widely known after being posted on the BBQ Addicts blog, and quickly spread to the mainstream press.

topic.name: Visual and Narrative Art in Media
topic.description: This topic encompasses the exploration of visual storytelling and artistic expression across various media forms, including painting, film, television, and pulp magazines.

Document 1: I Can See the Whole Room ... and There 's Nobody in It ! is a 1961 painting by Roy Lichtenstein.
The work is based on a William Overgard-drawn comics panel from a Steve Roper cartoon.
Document 2: The Terminator is a 1984 American science fiction action-thriller film written and directed by James Cameron.
It received critical acclaim, with many praising its pacing, action scenes and Schwarzenegger 's role.
Document 3: Flash Gordon Strange Adventure Magazine was a pulp magazine which was launched in December 1936.
It was an attempt to cash in on the growing comics boom, and the popularity of the Flash Gordon comic strip in particular.
Document 4: Flash Gordon is a science fiction television series based on the characters of the Alex Raymond-created comic strip.
The series set Flash, Dale Arden and Dr. Zarkov in the year 3203.
Document 5: Uncanny Tales was a Canadian science fiction pulp magazine edited by Melvin R. Colby that ran from November 1940 to September 1943.
It was created in response to the wartime reduction of imports on British and American science-fiction pulp magazines.
Document 6: Comet was a pulp magazine which published five issues from December 1940 to July 1941.
Tremaine paid one cent per word, which was higher than some of the competing magazines.
Document 7: Avatar: The Last Airbender is an American animated television series that aired for three seasons on Nickelodeon.
The show combines the styles of anime and American cartoons, and relies on the imagery of various East Asian, Inuit, Southeast Asian, South Asian, and New World societies.

topic.name: Mathematical Concepts and Functions
topic.description: The documents discuss various mathematical concepts and functions, including derivatives, matrices, logarithms, and specialized mathematical functions used in physics.

1: The derivative of a function of a real variable measures the sensitivity to change of a quantity.
Derivatives are a fundamental tool of calculus.
2: The Dirac delta function is a generalized function on the real number line that is zero everywhere except at zero.
It was introduced by theoretical physicist Paul Dirac.
3: A matrix is a rectangular array of numbers, symbols, or expressions, arranged in rows and columns.
A major application of matrices is to represent linear transformations.
4: A Hilbert space generalizes the notion of Euclidean space.
Hilbert spaces arise naturally and frequently in mathematics and physics.
5: The binary logarithm is the power to which the number 2 must be raised to obtain the value n.
Binary logarithms can be used to calculate the length of the representation of a number in the binary numeral system.
6: The weak interaction is one of the four known fundamental interactions of nature.
The weak interaction is responsible for radioactive decay.
7: The roots of the reconstructed Proto-Indo-European language are basic parts of words that carry a lexical meaning.
Complete inflected words like verbs, nouns or adjectives are formed by adding further morphemes to a root.

topic.name: Greek Biblical Manuscripts
topic.description: The documents primarily discuss various Greek manuscripts of the Bible, detailing their historical significance, textual content, and preservation status.

Document 1: fifth-century Greek manuscript of the Bible
contains material from every New Testament book except Second Thessalonians and Second John

dated palaeographically to the 6th century

contains the entire New Testament except the Book of Revelation

contains text of the four Gospels with unusual grammar forms and numerous errors

topic.name: Human Rights and Constitutional Law
topic.description: This cluster focuses on the intersection of human rights, constitutional law, and the mechanisms for protecting individual rights within various legal frameworks.

Document 1: International Covenant on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights (ICESCR) commits its parties to work toward the granting of economic, social, and cultural rights.
The ICESCR is part of the International Bill of Human Rights.
Document 2: The Constitution of the Republic of Singapore Tribunal was established to interpret constitutional questions regarding the validity of laws.
Questions referred to the Tribunal may concern the validity of enacted laws or bills.
Document 3: The Fourth Amendment prohibits unreasonable searches and seizures and requires warrants to be supported by probable cause.
It is part of the Bill of Rights adopted in response to abuse of governmental power.
Document 4: The Twenty-fifth Amendment deals with presidential succession and procedures for filling a vacancy in the office of the Vice President.
It clarifies the constitutional ambiguities regarding presidential disabilities.
Document 7: Planned Parenthood provides reproductive health services and advocates for reproductive rights, contributing to the discourse on individual rights.
It is the largest single provider of reproductive health services, including abortion, in the United States.

topic.name: Social Movements and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses various historical figures and movements that sought social change, including women's rights, religious reform, and cultural expressions.

1: Elizabeth Cady Stanton was a leading figure of the early women's rights movement.
Her Declaration of Sentiments is often credited with initiating the first organized women's rights and women's suffrage movements in the United States.
4: Breckinridge became involved in a number of theological debates.
He played an influential role in the ejection of several churches during the Old School-New School Controversy within the Presbyterian Church.

topic.name: Corporate Governance and Scandals

Document 1: Olympus scandal was precipitated on 14 October 2011 when British-born Michael Woodford was suddenly ousted as chief executive
the incident raised concern about the endurance of tobashi schemes, and the strength of corporate governance in Japan.

the bank's founder, Muhammad Yunus, were jointly awarded the Nobel Peace Prize.

the company served over 3.2 million clients primarily in the U.S.
